title.zh: 卷三十八 高五王傳
title.en: Volume 38: The five sons of Emperor Gao
title.raw: 卷三十八 高五王傳 Volume 38: The five sons of Emperor Gao

sentences.zh: 高皇帝八男：呂后生孝惠帝，曹夫人生齊悼惠王肥，薄姬生孝文帝，戚夫人生趙隱王如意，趙姬生淮南厲王長，諸姬生趙幽王友、趙共王恢、燕靈王建。

translations.literal: The August Emperor Gao had eight sons: Empress Lü bore Emperor Xiaohui; Lady Cao bore Prince Daohui of Qi, Fei; Consort Bo bore Emperor Xiaowen; Lady Qi bore the Hidden Prince of Zhao, Ruyi; Lady Zhao bore the Fierce Prince of Huainan, Chang; other ladies bore Prince You of Zhao the Obscure, Prince Hui of Zhao the Joint, and Prince Jian of Yan the Numinous.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gao fathered eight sons: Empress Lü gave him Emperor Xiaohui; Lady Cao gave him Prince Daohui of Qi, Liu Fei; Consort Bo bore the future Emperor Wen; Lady Qi bore Liu Ruyi, later titled the Hidden Prince of Zhao; Lady Zhao bore Liu Chang, the Fierce Prince of Huainan; and lesser consorts bore Liu You, Liu Hui, and Liu Jian, the Obscure Prince of Zhao, the Joint Prince of Zhao, and the Numinous Prince of Yan respectively.

sentences.zh: 淮南厲王長自有傳。

translations.literal: The Fierce Prince of Huainan, Chang, has his own biography.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Chang, the Fierce Prince of Huainan, is treated in a separate biography.

sentences.zh: 齊悼惠王

translations.literal: Prince Daohui of Qi
translations.idiomatic: Prince Daohui of Qi

sentences.zh: 趙隱王

translations.literal: The Hidden Prince of Zhao
translations.idiomatic: The Hidden Prince of Zhao

sentences.zh: 趙隱王如意，九年立。

translations.literal: The Hidden Prince of Zhao, Ruyi, was established in the ninth year.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ruyi, posthumously known as the Hidden Prince of Zhao, was invested as king in the ninth year of Gaozu's reign.

sentences.zh: 四年，高祖崩，呂太后徵王到長安，鴆殺之。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, the August Ancestor Gao died; Empress Dowager Lü summoned the king to Chang'an and killed him with poisoned wine.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Emperor Hui's reign, after Gaozu had died, Empress Dowager Lü summoned Prince Ruyi to Chang'an and murdered him with poisoned wine.

sentences.zh: 無子，絕。

translations.literal: He had no sons; the line was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: He left no heir, and his house came to an end.

sentences.zh: 趙幽王

translations.literal: Prince You of Zhao the Obscure
translations.idiomatic: Prince You of Zhao the Obscure

sentences.zh: 趙王遂立二十六年，孝景時晁錯以過削趙常山郡，諸侯怨，吳楚反，遂與合謀起兵。

translations.literal: King Liu Sui of Zhao had reigned twenty-six years. In the time of Emperor Xiaojing, Chao Cuo, citing faults, stripped Zhao of the Changshan commandery; the feudal lords resented it; Wu and Chu rebelled, and Sui joined them in plotting to raise troops.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Sui had been king of Zhao for twenty-six years when, under Emperor Jing, Chao Cuo seized the Changshan commandery from Zhao as punishment for alleged wrongdoing. The kingdoms were already smoldering with resentment; when Wu and Chu rose in revolt, Liu Sui conspired with them and mobilized his own forces.

sentences.zh: 其相建德、內史王悍諫，不聽。

translations.literal: His chancellor Jiande and his interior secretary Wang Han remonstrated; he would not listen.
translations.idiomatic: His chancellor Jiande and interior secretary Wang Han urged him to reconsider, but he refused to heed them.

sentences.zh: 遂燒殺德、悍，發兵住其西界，欲待吳楚俱進，北使匈奴與連和。

translations.literal: Sui then had Jiande and Han burned to death, sent troops to hold his western frontier, wished to wait until Wu and Chu advanced together, and sent envoys north to the Xiongnu to conclude an alliance.
translations.idiomatic: He had Jiande and Wang Han executed by fire, deployed troops along his western border to await a joint advance with Wu and Chu, and sent a mission north to court an alliance with the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 漢使曲周侯酈寄擊之，趙王城守邯鄲，相距七月。

translations.literal: The Han dispatched Marquis of Quzhou Li Ji to attack him; the King of Zhao defended Handan; they faced each other seven months.
translations.idiomatic: The court sent Marquis of Quzhou Li Ji against him. The king of Zhao shut himself inside Handan, and the siege lasted seven months.

sentences.zh: 吳楚敗，匈奴聞之，亦不肯入邊。

translations.literal: When Wu and Chu were defeated, the Xiongnu heard of it and would not enter the border either.
translations.idiomatic: After Wu and Chu collapsed, the Xiongnu, learning the outcome, likewise declined to cross the frontier.

sentences.zh: 欒布自破齊還，并兵引水灌趙城。

translations.literal: Luan Bu, returning from having crushed Qi, combined forces, led water in, and flooded the Zhao city.
translations.idiomatic: Luan Bo, returning from the campaign that had broken Qi, joined his command with others, diverted a river, and inundated the Zhao capital.

sentences.zh: 城壞，王遂自殺，國除。

translations.literal: The walls gave way; King Sui killed himself; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The ramparts failed; Liu Sui took his own life, and the kingdom was abolished.

sentences.zh: 景帝憐趙相、內史守正死，皆封其子為列侯。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing pitied the Zhao chancellor and interior secretary who had died upholding what was right, and enfeoffed both their sons as full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Touched by the loyalty of the Zhao chancellor and interior secretary who had died for their principles, Emperor Jing made each man's son a full marquis.

sentences.zh: 趙共王

translations.literal: Prince Hui of Zhao the Joint
translations.idiomatic: Prince Hui of Zhao the Joint

sentences.zh: 趙共王恢。

translations.literal: Prince Hui of Zhao, Hui.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Hui, the Joint Prince of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 十一年，梁王彭越誅，立恢為梁王。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, King Peng Yue of Liang was executed, and Hui was established as king of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year of Gaozu's reign, after Peng Yue, king of Liang, was put to death, Liu Hui was transferred to Liang and invested there.

sentences.zh: 十六年，趙幽王死，呂后徙恢王趙，恢心不樂。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Prince You of Zhao the Obscure died; Empress Lü moved Hui to be king of Zhao; Hui's heart was not glad.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year, when Prince You of Zhao died, Empress Lü reassigned Liu Hui to Zhao. He went unwillingly, his heart heavy at the move.

sentences.zh: 太后以呂產女為趙王后，王后從官皆諸呂也，內擅權，微司趙王，王不得自恣。

translations.literal: The empress dowager took Lü Chan's daughter as queen of Zhao; the queen's attendants were all of the Lü clan; within they monopolized power, secretly watched the king of Zhao, and the king could not follow his own will.
translations.idiomatic: She married him to a daughter of Lü Chan, whose entire household staff were Lü partisans. They seized the inner court, spied on the king at every turn, and left him unable to live as he pleased.

sentences.zh: 王有愛姬，王后鴆殺之。

translations.literal: The king had a beloved concubine; the queen killed her with poisoned wine.
translations.idiomatic: When he favored another woman, his Lü queen poisoned her.

sentences.zh: 王乃為歌詩四章，令樂人歌之。

translations.literal: The king then composed four stanzas of song-poetry and ordered musicians to sing them.
translations.idiomatic: In his grief he wrote four songs and had the court musicians perform them.

sentences.zh: 王悲思，六月自殺。

translations.literal: The king was sorrowful and brooding; in the sixth month he killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Brokenhearted, he took his own life in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 太后聞之，以為用婦人故自殺，無思奉宗廟禮，廢其嗣。

translations.literal: When the empress dowager heard of it, she thought that because of a woman he had killed himself, without thought for the rites owed the ancestral temple; she abolished his posterity.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager dismissed his death as the act of a man who had killed himself over a woman and showed no regard for his duty to the imperial ancestors; she struck his line from the succession.

sentences.zh: 燕靈王

translations.literal: Prince Jian of Yan the Numinous
translations.idiomatic: Prince Jian of Yan the Numinous

sentences.zh: 齊悼惠王子，前後凡九人為王：太子襄為齊哀王，次子章為城陽景王，興居為濟北王，將閭為齊王，志為濟北王，辟光為濟南王，賢為菑川王，卬為膠西王，雄渠為膠東王。

translations.literal: The sons of Prince Daohui of Qi, in all before and after, nine men became kings: the heir Xiang was Prince Ai of Qi; the second son Zhang was Prince Jing of Chengyang; Xingju was king of Jibei; Jianglü was king of Qi; Zhi was king of Jibei; Piguang was king of Jinan; Xian was king of Zichuan; Ang was king of Jiaoxi; Xiongqu was king of Jiaodong.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Daohui of Qi left nine sons who in turn received royal titles: the crown prince Xiang became Prince Ai of Qi; Liu Zhang became Prince Jing of Chengyang; Liu Xingju, king of Jibei; Liu Jianglü, king of Qi; Liu Zhi, again king of Jibei; Liu Piguang of Jinan; Liu Xian of Zichuan; Liu Ang of Jiaoxi; and Liu Xiongqu of Jiaodong.

sentences.zh: 齊哀王

translations.literal: Prince Ai of Qi
translations.idiomatic: Prince Ai of Qi

sentences.zh: 章年二十，有氣力，忿劉氏不得職。

translations.literal: Zhang was twenty years old, had strength and vigor, and resented that the Liu house did not hold office.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhang was twenty, strong and hot-tempered, and bitter that real power lay not with the house of Liu.

sentences.zh: 嘗入侍燕飲，高后令章為酒吏。

translations.literal: Once when he entered attendance at a banquet of drinking and entertainment, Empress Gao ordered Zhang to be wine steward.
translations.idiomatic: On one occasion he attended one of Empress Lü's feasts, and she put him in charge of the wine.

sentences.zh: 章自請曰：「臣，將種也，請得以軍法行酒。」

translations.literal: Zhang himself requested, saying, "I, your servant, am of a general's stock; I beg to conduct the wine by military law."
translations.idiomatic: He volunteered: "I am a soldier's son. Permit me to enforce the drinking rules as if we were under military law."

sentences.zh: 高后曰：「可。」

translations.literal: Empress Gao said: "It may be so."
translations.idiomatic: "Very well," said Empress Lü.

sentences.zh: 酒酣，章進歌舞，已而曰：「請為太后言耕田。」

translations.literal: When the wine was warm, Zhang advanced song and dance, and afterward said: "I beg to speak to the empress dowager of plowing fields."
translations.idiomatic: When spirits were high, he rose to offer a song and dance, then said he wished to tell the empress dowager a story about farming.

sentences.zh: 高后兒子畜之，笑曰：「顧乃父知田耳，若生而為王子，安知田乎？」

translations.literal: Empress Gao doted on him as a child and laughed, saying: "Only your father knew fields; you were born a king's son—how would you know fields?"
translations.idiomatic: She doted on him like a son and laughed. "Your father knew the plow; you were born a prince of the blood. What could you know of the soil?"

sentences.zh: 章曰：「臣知之。」

translations.literal: Zhang said: "Your servant knows it."
translations.idiomatic: "I know it," said Liu Zhang.

sentences.zh: 太后曰：「試為我言田意。」

translations.literal: The empress dowager said: "Try for me to speak the meaning of fields."
translations.idiomatic: "Then tell me what you know of the field," she said.

sentences.zh: 章曰：「深耕穊種，立苗欲疏；

translations.literal: Zhang said: "Deep plowing and dense sowing; setting seedlings one desires sparse;
translations.idiomatic: "Plow deep and sow thick," he began; "set the seedlings far apart—"

sentences.zh: 非其種者，鉏而去之。」

translations.literal: What is not of its kind, hoe it out and remove it."
translations.idiomatic: "—and whatever is not your own strain, hoe it up and cast it away."

sentences.zh: 太后默然。

translations.literal: The empress dowager was silent.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager fell silent, the barb having struck home.

sentences.zh: 頃之，諸呂有一人醉，亡酒，章追，拔劍斬之，而還報曰：「有亡酒一人，臣謹行軍法斬之。」

translations.literal: Before long, one of the Lü men was drunk, fled the wine, Zhang pursued him, drew his sword and beheaded him, and returning reported: "There was one who fled the wine; your servant, respectfully applying military law, has beheaded him."
translations.idiomatic: Presently one of the Lü guests broke the rules and tried to slip away. Liu Zhang ran him down, cut off his head, and reported back: "One man deserted his cup. I have executed him under military law."

sentences.zh: 太后左右大驚。

translations.literal: Those to the left and right of the empress dowager were greatly startled.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager's attendants cried out in alarm.

sentences.zh: 業已許其軍法，亡以罪也。

translations.literal: Already they had permitted him military law; there was no way to punish him.
translations.idiomatic: She had already authorized military discipline; there was no honest pretext on which to punish him.

sentences.zh: 因罷酒。

translations.literal: Thereupon they stopped the wine.
translations.idiomatic: The banquet broke up at once.

sentences.zh: 自是後，諸呂憚章，雖大臣皆依朱虛侯。

translations.literal: From this afterward, the Lü feared Zhang; even great ministers leaned on the Marquis of Zhuxu.
translations.idiomatic: After that the Lü clan feared Liu Zhang, and even senior ministers looked to the Marquis of Zhuxu for leadership.

sentences.zh: 劉氏為彊。

translations.literal: The Liu house became strong.
translations.idiomatic: The house of Liu gathered strength again.

sentences.zh: 其明年，高后崩。

translations.literal: The next year, Empress Gao died.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Empress Lü died.

sentences.zh: 趙王呂祿為上將軍，呂王產為相國，皆居長安中，聚兵以威大臣，欲為亂。

translations.literal: King Lü Lu of Zhao was senior general; King Lü Chan was chancellor; both dwelt in Chang'an, gathered troops to awe the great ministers, and wished to make rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Lu, king of Zhao, held the post of senior general; Lü Chan, king of Lü, was chancellor. Both remained in Chang'an, massed troops to overawe the ministers, and plotted revolt.

sentences.zh: 章以呂祿女為婦，知其謀，乃使人陰出告其兄齊王，欲令發兵西，朱虛侯、東牟侯欲從中與大臣為內應，以誅諸呂，因立齊王為帝。

translations.literal: Zhang took Lü Lu's daughter as wife, knew their plot, then sent men secretly out to inform his brother the king of Qi, wishing to have him raise troops westward; the Marquis of Zhuxu and the Marquis of Dongmou wished from within to join with the great ministers as inner response, in order to execute the Lü, and thereby establish the king of Qi as emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Because Zhang was married to Lü Lu's daughter, he learned of the conspiracy and secretly warned his brother, the king of Qi, urging him to march west while he and his cousin, the Marquis of Dongmou, coordinated with loyal ministers inside the capital to extirpate the Lü and set the king of Qi on the throne.

sentences.zh: 齊王聞此計，與其舅駟鈞、郎中令祝午、中尉魏勃陰謀發兵。

translations.literal: The king of Qi heard this plan, and with his maternal uncle Si Jun, palace superintendent Zhu Wu, and commandant Wei Bo secretly plotted to raise troops.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi took counsel in secret with his uncle Si Jun, his palace superintendent Zhu Wu, and commandant Wei Bo.

sentences.zh: 齊相召平聞之，乃發兵入衛王宮。

translations.literal: The chancellor of Qi, Shao Ping, heard of it, then raised troops to enter and guard the royal palace.
translations.idiomatic: When Chancellor Shao Ping of Qi got wind of it, he called out the guard and sealed the royal palace.

sentences.zh: 魏勃紿平曰：「王欲發兵，非有漢虎符驗也。

translations.literal: Wei Bo deceived Ping, saying: "The king wishes to raise troops, yet there is no verification by Han tiger tallies.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bo lied to him: "The king means to rebel without the court's tiger tallies.

sentences.zh: 而相君圍王，固善。

translations.literal: Yet the chancellor besieges the king—indeed it is good.
translations.idiomatic: You have wisely surrounded him.

sentences.zh: 勃請為君將兵衛衛王。」

translations.literal: Bo begs to take for you command of troops to guard the king."
translations.idiomatic: Let me relieve you: give me the troops, and I will garrison the palace in your stead."

sentences.zh: 召平信之，乃使魏勃將。

translations.literal: Shao Ping trusted him, then sent Wei Bo to command.
translations.idiomatic: Shao Ping believed him and handed over the command.

sentences.zh: 勃既將，以兵圍相府。

translations.literal: Bo, having taken command, used troops to surround the chancellor's headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bo at once turned the same troops against the chancellor's compound and besieged it.

sentences.zh: 召平曰：「嗟乎！

translations.literal: Shao Ping said: "Alas!
translations.idiomatic: "Alas," cried Shao Ping,

sentences.zh: 道家之言『當斷不斷，反受其亂』。」

translations.literal: The words of the Daoists: 'When one ought to decide and does not, one instead receives disorder.'"
translations.idiomatic: "the Daoists had it right: hesitate when you must act, and chaos finds you instead."

sentences.zh: 遂自殺。

translations.literal: Thereupon he killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: He killed himself on the spot.

sentences.zh: 於是齊王以駟鈞為相，魏勃為將軍，祝午為內史，悉發國中兵。

translations.literal: Thereupon the king of Qi took Si Jun as chancellor, Wei Bo as general, Zhu Wu as interior secretary, and fully raised the troops of the state.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi then named Si Jun chancellor, Wei Bo general, and Zhu Wu interior secretary, and mobilized every able-bodied man in the kingdom.

sentences.zh: 使祝午紿琅邪王曰：「呂氏為亂，齊王發兵欲西誅之。

translations.literal: He sent Zhu Wu to deceive the king of Langye, saying: "The Lü are in rebellion; the king of Qi has raised troops wishing to go west and execute them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Wu was sent with a tale for the king of Langye: "The Lü have turned traitor; the king of Qi is marching west to punish them.

sentences.zh: 齊王自以兒子，年少，不習兵革之事，願舉國委大王。

translations.literal: The king of Qi, considering himself a young son, young in years, not practiced in matters of armor and weapons, wishes to entrust the whole state to the great king.
translations.idiomatic: He is young and untried in war and begs to place the entire army under your command.

sentences.zh: 大王自高帝將也，習戰事。

translations.literal: The great king from the time of the August Emperor was a general, practiced in warfare.
translations.idiomatic: You commanded troops for Emperor Gao and know the field.

sentences.zh: 齊王不敢離兵，使臣請大王幸之臨菑見齊王計事，并將齊兵以西平關中之亂。」

translations.literal: The king of Qi does not dare leave the troops; he sends your servant to request the great king, if you would be so kind, to come to Linzi to meet the king of Qi to plan affairs, and together lead the troops of Qi west to pacify the disorders within the passes."
translations.idiomatic: He cannot leave his host, so he asks you to ride to Linzi to take supreme command and lead the combined forces of Qi and Langye west to settle the turmoil in Guanzhong."

sentences.zh: 琅邪王信之，以為然，乃馳見齊王。

translations.literal: The king of Langye trusted it, thought it so, then galloped to see the king of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Langye believed every word and sped to Linzi.

sentences.zh: 齊王與魏勃等因留琅邪王，而使祝午盡發琅邪國而并將其兵。

translations.literal: The king of Qi, with Wei Bo and others, therefore detained the king of Langye, and sent Zhu Wu to fully raise Langye's state and together command its troops.
translations.idiomatic: There Liu Zhang and his confederates held him fast while Zhu Wu stripped Langye of its remaining troops and brought them under Qi's banner.

sentences.zh: 琅邪王劉澤既欺，不得反國，乃說齊王曰：「齊悼惠王，高皇帝長子也，推本言之，大王高皇帝適長孫也，當立。

translations.literal: King Liu Ze of Langye, having been deceived, could not return to his state, then persuaded the king of Qi, saying: "Prince Daohui of Qi was the August Emperor Gao's eldest son; tracing the root in speaking, the great king is the August Emperor Gao's eldest grandson by the proper wife; he ought to be established.
translations.idiomatic: Trapped in Qi, Liu Ze argued: "Prince Daohui was Gaozu's eldest son; by strict descent you are Gaozu's senior grandson by the main line and have the strongest claim to the throne.

sentences.zh: 今諸大臣狐疑未有所定，而澤於劉氏最為長年，大臣固待澤決計。

translations.literal: Now the great ministers hesitate and have not yet decided, and Ze among the Liu is the eldest in years; the great ministers certainly await Ze to decide the plan.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers in Chang'an still waver, and I am the oldest living prince of the blood; they will listen when I speak.

sentences.zh: 今大王留臣無為也，不如使我入關計事。」

translations.literal: Now the great king detaining your servant accomplishes nothing; it would be better to let me enter the passes to plan affairs."
translations.idiomatic: Keeping me here serves no purpose. Send me through the passes to settle the succession in your favor."

sentences.zh: 齊王以為然，乃益具車送琅邪王。

translations.literal: The king of Qi thought it so, then increased carriages and saw off the king of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi agreed, furnished him with a grand escort, and sent him on his way.

sentences.zh: 漢聞之，相國呂產等遣大將軍潁陰侯灌嬰將兵擊之。

translations.literal: When the Han heard of it, Chancellor Lü Chan and others dispatched Grand General Marquis of Yingyin Guan Ying to lead troops and attack him.
translations.idiomatic: The court learned of the rising and ordered Grand General Guan Ying, Marquis of Yingyin, to take the field against Qi.

sentences.zh: 嬰至滎陽，乃謀曰：「諸呂舉兵關中，欲危劉氏而自立，今我破齊還報，是益呂氏資也。」

translations.literal: When Ying reached Xingyang, he plotted, saying: "The Lü have raised troops within the passes, wishing to endanger the Liu and establish themselves; now if I break Qi and return to report, this increases the Lü's resources."
translations.idiomatic: At Xingyang he halted and reasoned with his officers: "The Lü hold Guanzhong and mean to supplant the Liu. If I crush Qi and march home, I only strengthen their hand."

sentences.zh: 乃留兵屯滎陽，使人諭齊王及諸侯，與連和，以待呂氏之變而共誅之。

translations.literal: Then he left troops garrisoned at Xingyang, sent men to instruct the king of Qi and the feudal lords, joined in alliance with them, and waited for the Lü's coup in order together to execute them.
translations.idiomatic: He camped at Xingyang, sent envoys to the king of Qi and the other kings, and forged a common front to wait for the Lü to move, then strike them down together.

sentences.zh: 齊王聞之，乃屯兵西界待約。

translations.literal: The king of Qi heard it, then garrisoned troops on the western border awaiting the agreement.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi drew up his army on his western frontier to await the signal.

sentences.zh: 呂祿、呂產欲作亂，朱虛侯章與太尉勃、丞相平等誅之。

translations.literal: Lü Lu and Lü Chan wished to make rebellion; Marquis of Zhuxu Zhang with Grand Commandant Bo, Chancellor Ping, and others executed them.
translations.idiomatic: When Lü Lu and Lü Chan moved toward open revolt, Liu Zhang joined Zhou Bo, Chen Ping, and the loyal ministers in cutting them down.

sentences.zh: 章首先斬呂產，太尉勃等乃盡誅諸呂。

translations.literal: Zhang first beheaded Lü Chan; Grand Commandant Bo and others then fully executed the Lü.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhang struck the first blow, killing Lü Chan; Zhou Bo and the rest then wiped out the entire Lü faction.

sentences.zh: 而琅邪王亦從齊至長安。

translations.literal: And the king of Langye also came from Qi to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Liu Ze had traveled from Qi to Chang'an as well.

sentences.zh: 大臣議欲立齊王，皆曰：「母家駟鈞惡戾，虎而冠者也。

translations.literal: The great ministers deliberated wishing to establish the king of Qi; all said: "The mother's family Si Jun is cruel and perverse, a tiger wearing a cap.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers first considered the king of Qi, but objected in one voice: "His uncle Si Jun is vicious and overbearing—a man with a tiger's heart beneath a courtier's hat.

sentences.zh: 訪以呂氏故，幾亂天下，今又立齊王，是欲復為呂氏也。

translations.literal: By the cause of the Lü, they nearly threw the realm into chaos; now again to establish the king of Qi is to wish again to make a Lü."
translations.idiomatic: The Lü rose from a mother's clan and nearly destroyed the dynasty; to enthrone Qi now would be to invite another Lü."

sentences.zh: 代王母家薄氏，君子長者，且代王，高帝子，於今見在，最為長。

translations.literal: The king of Dai's mother's family, the Bo, are gentlemen and elders; moreover the king of Dai, a son of August Emperor Gao, among those now living is the eldest.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Dai's kin on his mother's side, the Bo, are modest and trustworthy; he himself is Gaozu's son and the eldest prince still living.

sentences.zh: 以子則順，以善人則大臣安。」

translations.literal: As to sons it is compliant; as to good men the great ministers are at peace."
translations.idiomatic: Succession by seniority favors him, and a good in-law clan would steady the ministers."

sentences.zh: 於是大臣乃謀迎代王，而遣章以誅呂氏事告齊王，令罷兵。

translations.literal: Thereupon the great ministers then plotted to welcome the king of Dai, and dispatched Zhang to inform the king of Qi of the affair of executing the Lü, ordering him to disband the troops.
translations.idiomatic: They therefore resolved to welcome Liu Heng of Dai, sent Liu Zhang to tell his brother in Qi that the Lü were dead, and ordered him to stand down.

sentences.zh: 灌嬰在滎陽，聞魏勃本教齊王反，既誅呂氏，罷齊兵，使使召責問魏勃。

translations.literal: Guan Ying was at Xingyang; he heard that Wei Bo had originally instructed the king of Qi to rebel; after the Lü had been executed and Qi troops disbanded, he sent envoys to summon and rebuke Wei Bo.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Ying, still at Xingyang, had heard that Wei Bo had instigated the king of Qi's revolt. Once the Lü were gone and Qi's army sent home, he summoned Wei Bo to account for himself.

sentences.zh: 勃曰：「失火之家，豈暇先言丈人後救火乎！」

translations.literal: Bo said: "In a house on fire, how is there leisure first to speak to the elders and afterward fight the fire!"
translations.idiomatic: "When the roof is burning," Wei Bo replied, "do you first bow to your elders or grab a bucket?"

sentences.zh: 因退立，股戰而栗。

translations.literal: Thereupon he withdrew and stood; his thighs trembled and he shivered.
translations.idiomatic: He stepped back, legs trembling, unable to steady himself.

sentences.zh: 恐不能言者，終無他語。

translations.literal: Fearing he could not speak, in the end he had no other words.
translations.idiomatic: Too frightened to say more, he offered no further defense.

sentences.zh: 灌將軍孰視，笑曰：「人謂魏勃勇，妄庸人耳，何能為乎！」

translations.literal: General Guan stared long, laughed, and said: "People call Wei Bo brave—he is merely a foolish common fellow; what can he do!"
translations.idiomatic: Guan Ying studied him a long moment, then laughed. "They say Wei Bo is bold. The man is a panicky fool—harmless."

sentences.zh: 乃罷勃。

translations.literal: Thereupon he dismissed Bo.
translations.idiomatic: He let Wei Bo go.

sentences.zh: 勃父以善鼓琴見秦皇帝。

translations.literal: Bo's father, by being good at drumming the zither, was received by the Qin August Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bo's father had won an audience with the First Emperor of Qin by his skill on the zither.

sentences.zh: 及勃少時，欲求見齊相曹參，家貧無以自通，乃常獨早埽齊相舍人門外。

translations.literal: When Bo was young, he wished to seek an audience with Chancellor Cao Shen of Qi; his family was poor and had no means to introduce himself; therefore he often alone early swept outside the gate of the chancellor of Qi's retainer.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth, wanting to meet Chancellor Cao Shen of Qi but too poor for an introduction, he rose before dawn day after day to sweep the doorstep of one of the chancellor's retainers.

sentences.zh: 舍人怪之，以為物而司之，得勃。

translations.literal: The retainer thought it strange, thought it a spirit and watched for it, and caught Bo.
translations.idiomatic: The retainer thought some ghost was at work and lay in wait until he caught Wei Bo in the act.

sentences.zh: 勃曰：「願見相君，無因，故為子埽，欲以求見。」

translations.literal: Bo said: "I wish to see the chancellor; having no means of access, therefore I sweep for you, wishing thereby to seek an audience."
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bo said, "I beg an audience with the chancellor, but I have no way in. That is why I have been sweeping your doorstep—to earn a chance to meet him."

sentences.zh: 齊王既罷兵歸，而代王立，是為孝文帝。

translations.literal: The king of Qi having disbanded the troops and returned, the king of Dai was established; this is Emperor Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi sent his men home; Liu Heng of Dai ascended the throne as Emperor Wen.

sentences.zh: 城陽景王章，孝文二年以朱虛侯與東牟侯興居俱立，二年薨。

translations.literal: Prince Jing of Chengyang, Zhang, in the second year of Xiaowen, together with Marquis of Zhuxu and Marquis of Dongmou Xingju was established; in the second year he died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhang, Prince Jing of Chengyang, was enfeoffed in the second year of Emperor Wen together with his brother Xingju, Marquis of Dongmou; he died two years later.

sentences.zh: 子共王喜嗣。

translations.literal: The son, Prince Gong Xi, succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Liu Xi, Prince Gong of Chengyang, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 孝文十二年，徙王淮南，五年，復還王城陽，凡立三十三年薨。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year of Xiaowen, he was moved to be king of Huainan; in the fifth year, again returned to be king of Chengyang; in all he reigned thirty-three years and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year of Emperor Wen he was transferred to Huainan; five years after that he was brought back to Chengyang. He reigned thirty-three years in all before he died.

sentences.zh: 子頃王延嗣，二十六年薨。

translations.literal: The son, Prince Qing Yan, succeeded; in twenty-six years he died.
translations.idiomatic: His son Liu Yan, Prince Qing of Chengyang, reigned twenty-six years.

sentences.zh: 子敬王義嗣，九年薨。

translations.literal: The son, Prince Jing Yi, succeeded; in nine years he died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yi, Prince Jing, followed and reigned nine years.

sentences.zh: 子惠王武嗣，十一年薨。

translations.literal: The son, Prince Hui Wu, succeeded; in eleven years he died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Wu, Prince Hui, held the throne eleven years.

sentences.zh: 子荒王順嗣，四十六年薨。

translations.literal: The son, Prince Huang Shun, succeeded; in forty-six years he died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Shun, Prince Huang, ruled forty-six years.

sentences.zh: 子戴王恢嗣，八年薨。

translations.literal: The son, Prince Dai Hui, succeeded; in eight years he died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Hui, Prince Dai, succeeded him and died after eight years.

sentences.zh: 子孝王景嗣，二十四年薨。
子哀王雲嗣，一年薨，無子，國絕。
成帝復立雲兄俚為城陽王，王莽時絕。
濟北王興居初以東牟侯與大臣共立文帝於代邸，曰：「誅呂氏，臣無功，請與太僕滕公俱入清宮。」
遂將少帝出，迎皇帝入宮。
立十一年，孝景三年，吳楚反，膠東、膠西、菑川、濟南王皆發兵應吳楚。
欲與齊，齊孝王狐疑，城守不聽。
三國兵共圍齊，齊王使路中大夫告於天子。
天子復令路中大夫還報，告齊王堅守，漢兵今破吳楚矣。
路中大夫至，三國兵圍臨菑數重，無從入。
三國將與路中大夫盟曰：「若反言漢已破矣，齊趣下三國，不且見屠。」
路中大夫既許，至城下，望見齊王，曰：「漢已發兵百萬，使太尉亞夫擊破吳楚，方引兵救齊，齊必堅守無下！」
三國將誅路中大夫。
齊初圍急，陰與三國通謀，約未定，會路中大夫從漢來，其大臣乃復勸王無下三國。
會漢將欒布、平陽侯等兵至齊，擊破三國兵，解圍。
已後聞齊初與三國有謀，將欲移兵伐齊。
齊孝王懼，飲藥自殺。
而膠東、膠西、濟南、菑川王皆伏誅，國除。
獨濟北王在。
其母曰紀太后。
太后取其弟紀氏女為王后，王不愛。
紀太后欲其家重寵，令其長女紀翁主入王宮正其後宮無令得近王，欲令愛紀氏女。
王因與其姊翁主姦。
齊有宦者徐甲，入事漢皇太后。
皇太后有愛女曰脩成君，脩成君非劉氏子，太后憐之。
脩成君有女娥，太后欲嫁之於諸侯。
宦者甲乃請使齊，必令王上書請娥。
皇太后大喜，使甲之齊。
時主父偃知甲之使齊以取后事，亦因謂甲：「即事成，幸言偃女願得充王後宮。」
甲至齊，風以此事。
紀太后怒曰：「王有后，後宮備具。
且甲，齊貧人，及為宦者入事漢，初無補益，乃欲亂吾王家！
且主父偃何為者？
乃欲以女充後宮！」
甲大窮，還報皇太后曰：「王已願尚娥，然事有所害，恐如燕王。」
燕王者，與其子昆弟姦，坐死。
故以燕感太后。
太后曰：「毋復言嫁女齊事。」
事寖淫聞於上。
主父偃由此與齊有隙。
偃方幸用事，因言：「齊臨菑十萬戶，巿租千金，人眾殷富，鉅於長安，非天子親弟愛子不得王此。
今齊王於親屬益疏。」
乃從容言呂太后時齊欲反，及吳楚時孝王幾為亂。
今聞齊王與其姊亂。
於是武帝拜偃為齊相，且正其事。
偃至齊，急治王後宮宦者為王通於姊翁主所者，辭及王。
王年少，懼以罪為吏所執誅，乃飲藥自殺。
是時趙王懼主父偃壹出敗齊，恐其漸疏骨肉，乃上書言偃受金及輕重之短，天子亦因囚偃。
公孫弘曰：「齊王以憂死，無後，非誅偃無以塞天下之望。」
偃遂坐誅。
濟北王
濟北王志，吳楚反時初亦與通謀，後堅守不發兵，故得不誅，徙王菑川。
元朔中，齊國絕。
悼惠王後唯有二國：城陽、菑川。
菑川地比齊，武帝為悼惠王冢園在齊，乃割臨菑東圜悼惠王冢園邑盡以予菑川，令奉祭祀。
志立三十五年薨，是為懿王。
子靖王建嗣，二十年薨。
子頃王遺嗣，三十五年薨。
子思王終古嗣。
五鳳中，青州刺史奏終古使所愛奴與八子及諸御婢姦，終古或參與被席，或白晝使羸伏，犬馬交接，終古親臨觀。
產子，輒曰：「亂不可知，使去其子。」
事下丞相御史，奏終古位諸侯王，以令置八子，秩比六百石，所以廣嗣重祖也。
而終古禽獸行，亂君臣夫婦之別，悖逆人倫，請逮捕。
有詔削四縣。
二十八年薨。
子考王尚嗣，五年薨。
子孝王橫嗣，三十一年薨。
子懷王交嗣，六年薨。
子永嗣，王莽時絕。
贊曰：悼惠之王齊，最為大國。
以海內初定，子弟少，激秦孤立亡藩輔，故大封同姓，以填天下。
時諸侯得自除御史大夫群卿以下眾官，如漢朝，漢獨為置丞相。
自吳楚誅後，稍奪諸侯權，左官附益阿黨之法設。
其後諸侯唯得衣食租稅，貧者或乘牛車。
